History
Arcane Acceleration was first introduced in the Trial mission The Law of Retribution, which was retired in Update 22.14 (2018-03-01).
The Operation: Scarlet Spear event sold Arcane Acceleration from Little Duck for 1,500  Scarlet Credits.
The Operation: Orphix Venom event sold Arcane Acceleration from Father for 300  Phasic Cells.
The Operation: Gargoyle's Cry event sold Arcane Acceleration from the Vigile Jahu Gargoyle for 5  Grotesque Splinter.
The Operation: Belly of the Beast event sold Arcane Acceleration from Ordis for 3  Volatile Motes in the first run of the event, and 4 Motes in the second, with a purchase limit of 42.
The Operation: Eight Claw event sold Arcane Acceleration from Dominus Thrax in bundles of 7, costing 2  Dominus Aureus, with a purchase limit of 3 (21 Arcanes in total).
Notes
Additively stacks with mods that increase fire rate (e.g.  Speed Trigger).
Does not double its bonus for bows
Bonus applies to most primary weapons, including snipers & launchers, but not shotguns.
Can be triggered by critical hits from almost any source, including all weapon types, beast and sentinel attacks, and even Warframe abilities (such as  Wisp's  Breach Surge sparks and all of  Gyre's damaging abilities)
Patch History

Update 31.6 (2022-06-09)

ARCANE STAT CHANGES PER RANK
We’ve adjusted the values of older Arcanes with the goal of making lower rank Arcanes more viable, and more desirable while collecting as a result. In short, there are three stats that each Arcane tends to have (speaking generally) -- for example, a percentage chance, a stat buff, and the duration of the buff.
Certain Arcanes increase all of these stats per rank (making them scale exponentially), but we are changing it so that only one stat is affected by rank (making it scale linearly). The other stats will now be at the max-rank value regardless of the Arcane Rank, thereby buffing lower-rank Arcanes as a result.

Arcane Acceleration
On Critical Hit: 30% (was 5%) chance for 15%/90% Fire Rate to Primary Weapons (excluding Shotguns) for 9s (was 2s)
Update 22.12 (2018-02-09)

Now has a base effect of 15% with a base chance of 5%.
Update 16.0 (2015-03-19)

Introduced.

History
Arcane Aegis was first introduced in the Trial mission The Jordas Verdict, which was retired in Update 22.14 (2018-03-01).
The Operation: Scarlet Spear event sold Arcane Aegis from Little Duck for 2,000  Scarlet Credits.
The Operation: Orphix Venom event sold Arcane Aegis from Father for 400  Phasic Cells.
The Operation: Gargoyle's Cry event sold Arcane Aegis from the Vigile Jahu Gargoyle for 6  Grotesque Splinter.
The Operation: Belly of the Beast event sold Arcane Aegis from Ordis for 5  Volatile Motes in the first run of the event, and 6 Motes in the second, with a purchase limit of 42.
The Operation: Eight Claw event sold Arcane Aegis from Dominus Thrax in bundles of 7, costing 4  Dominus Aureus, with a purchase limit of 3 (21 Arcanes total).
The Operation: Blood of Perita event sold Arcane Aegis from Roathe for 4  Marks of Valiance with a purchase limit of 21 per week.
Notes
Has a hidden stat that sets shield recharge delay to 0 seconds while active. This causes the players shields to constantly regenerate, even when being damaged.
 Magnetic status effect still prevents shield regeneration with this Arcane active.
Shield Recharge bonus is additive to the innate 5% shield recharge rate.
Shield recharge bonus stacks multiplicatively with  Fast Deflection and  Vigilante Vigor.
A  Hildryn with  Redirection,  Fast Deflection, and  Arcane Aegis equipped will have a shield recharge rate of
[ 15 + ( 5% +  30% ) * 1780 * ( 1 +  100% ) ] * (1 +  90%) = 2,395.9 shields per second while  Arcane Aegis is active.
Does not generate overshields.
Since this Arcane only triggers when shields are damaged, it has no benefit on  Inaros,  Kullervo, and  Nidus, as they have no shields to begin with, or during a Nightmare Mode mission with the No Shields modifier.
Bugs
Activating Arcane Aegis once will break the 80% Shield Recharge cap for the rest of the mission or until death.
After the cap is lifted,  Gauss's Passive,  Jade's  Symphony of Mercy, and  Vazarin ability  Guardian Break when paired with  Fast Deflection and  Vigilante Vigor will set Shield Recharge Delay to 0. Due to Shield Gating, this makes shields effectively impossible to bypass except through Toxin or Magnetic status.

Patch History

Update 31.6 (2022-06-09)

ARCANE STAT CHANGES PER RANK
We’ve adjusted the values of older Arcanes with the goal of making lower rank Arcanes more viable, and more desirable while collecting as a result. In short, there are three stats that each Arcane tends to have (speaking generally) -- for example, a percentage chance, a stat buff, and the duration of the buff.
Certain Arcanes increase all of these stats per rank (making them scale exponentially), but we are changing it so that only one stat is affected by rank (making it scale linearly). The other stats will now be at the max-rank value regardless of the Arcane Rank, thereby buffing lower-rank Arcanes as a result.

History
Arcane Avenger was first introduced in the Trial mission The Law of Retribution, which was retired in Update 22.14 (2018-03-01).
The Operation: Scarlet Spear event sold Arcane Avenger from Little Duck for 2,000  Scarlet Credits.
The Operation: Orphix Venom event sold Arcane Avenger from Father for 400  Phasic Cells.
The Operation: Gargoyle's Cry event sold Arcane Avenger from the Vigile Jahu Gargoyle for 6  Grotesque Splinter.
The Operation: Belly of the Beast event sold Arcane Avenger from Ordis for 5  Volatile Motes in the first run of the event, and 6 Motes in the second, with a purchase limit of 42.
The Operation: Eight Claw event sold Arcane Avenger from Dominus Thrax in bundles of 7, costing 4  Dominus Aureus, with a purchase limit of 3 (21 Arcanes in total).
Notes
Capable of making some abilities crit.
Main article: Universal Weapon Bonuses
A Rank 5 Arcane Avenger adds 45 percentage points to a weapon's critical chance after other bonuses.
Arcane Avenger stacks additively with other percentage point bonuses, such as  Covenant and  Cat's Eye. For example, an  Atterax with a max rank  True Steel,  Cat's Eye, and Arcane Avenger, will have a [25% × (1 + 120%) + 60% + 45%] = 160% critical chance on attacks.
Highly effective with weapons with high critical damage multipliers.
Weapons that have 0% critical chance can crit with this enhancement, although due to their lack of a critical multiplier, their damage will not be altered unless the shot lands on the head, which yields 2x critical multiplier.
Aside from damage to shields and health, this Arcane can be triggered by the following instances of damage:
Damage to player Overguard.
Self-damage from  Combat Discipline.
Damage while the player is invulnerable, such as  Absorb.
Damage that is rounded down to 0 due to damage reduction or redirection can trigger this Arcane.
For example, with a Rank 0  Combat Discipline equipped and  Eclipse active for 75% damage reduction, kills will deal [1 × (1 - 75%)] = 0.25 self-damage, which is rounded down to 0, but this Arcane can still trigger.
For example, with a Rank 2  Combat Discipline equipped while on  Merulina providing 90% damage redirection, kills will deal [4 × (1 - 90%)] = 0.4 self-damage, which is rounded down to 0, but this Arcane can still trigger.
Damage while  Mesmer Skin or  Mesmer Shield is active will not trigger this Arcane, unless  Combat Discipline is equipped.
Damage from  Despoil does not trigger this Arcane.

History
Arcane Awakening was first introduced in the Trial mission The Law of Retribution, which was retired in Update 22.14 (2018-03-01).
The Operation: Scarlet Spear event sold Arcane Awakening from Little Duck for 1,500  Scarlet Credits.
The Operation: Orphix Venom event sold Arcane Awakening from Father for 300  Phasic Cells.
The Operation: Gargoyle's Cry event sold Arcane Awakening from the Vigile Jahu Gargoyle for 5  Grotesque Splinter.
The Operation: Belly of the Beast event sold Arcane Awakening from Ordis for 3  Volatile Motes in the first run of the event, and 4 Motes in the second, with a purchase limit of 42.
The Operation: Eight Claw event sold Arcane Awakening from Dominus Thrax in bundles of 7, costing 2  Dominus Aureus, with a purchase limit of 3 (21 Arcanes in total).
Notes
The increase in damage stacks additively with mods that increase base damage e.g.  Hornet Strike.
Triggers at the end of the reload.
Weapons that cannot reload, such as Bows,  Epitaph, and Battery Weapons, cannot activate the buff. However, they can benefit from it once it has been activated by reloading a different weapon.
This also applies to  Mesa's  Regulators/ Regulators Prime.
Weapons that reload per shell have a chance of activating Awakening each time a shell is loaded.
The passive reload from mods like  Eject Magazine or the Synth Set will activate Awakening.
Bugs
Base  Regulators receive double the base damage bonus.  Regulators Prime work normally however.
Patch History

Hotfix 34.0.8 (2023-11-09)

Fixed Arcane Awakening buff description showing up as a file path.
Update 31.6 (2022-06-09)

ARCANE STAT CHANGES PER RANK
We’ve adjusted the values of older Arcanes with the goal of making lower rank Arcanes more viable, and more desirable while collecting as a result. In short, there are three stats that each Arcane tends to have (speaking generally) -- for example, a percentage chance, a stat buff, and the duration of the buff.
Certain Arcanes increase all of these stats per rank (making them scale exponentially), but we are changing it so that only one stat is affected by rank (making it scale linearly). The other stats will now be at the max-rank value regardless of the Arcane Rank, thereby buffing lower-rank Arcanes as a result.

Patch History

Update 36.0 (2024-06-18)

Trading Legendary Arcanes now requires you to be Mastery Rank 11.
It’s important to note that only the player trading requires MR 11 and not the receiver.
We needed to address an issue with exploitative Legendary Arcanes farming and ban-evasion account creation used to trade Legendary Arcanes. Other options we experimented with had the risk of innocent players getting caught in the crossfire of the ban-evasion subjects. When we took a look at Trading stats we found that a very small number of players below Mastery Rank 10 trade Legendary Arcanes, and therefore decided to go this route. This is purely a means of deterring the bad actors and ensuring legitimate trading for players.
Update 27.2 (2020-03-05)

Added 2 additional ranks for a maximum of 6% chance to activate from a 4% chance to activate.
Now specifies On Shield Damage (can no longer activate when receiving damage to health).
Update 16.0 (2015-03-19)

Introduced.

Notes
Maximum energy changes dynamically based on any changes to armor.
As such, being inflicted with a  Corrosive or  Heat status effect will remove armor and some of your bonus energy, requiring you to regain it.
 Heat procs are far more common and more deadly, as they will remove up to half of your total armor while burning.
 Health Conversion is unwieldy on Simulacrum and might look like it isn't properly updating, but it does work mostly reliably in normal missions.
If armor is reduced, such as losing a stack of Health Conversion, any energy overflow will immediately disappear.
When combined with  Preparation or similar effects, the increased energy maximum is applied first, resulting in a higher starting energy as well.
Tips
Works well with mods like  Quick Thinking and  Rage.
 Qorvex,  Valkyr Prime, and  Kullervo have high base armor that grants them effective use of this arcane.

Notes
Ability Strength bonus does not scale per every 250 maximum health, and instead uses the following: round(Max Health÷250×Strength Increase)
This means the required maximum health is 2,980 instead of 3,000.
Compared to  Molt Augmented, this Arcane's Ability Strength bonus is constantly active even if the player uses a Revive, but is highly dependent on the given Warframe's maximum health.
Assuming both Arcanes are at max rank, Arcane Bellicose will require 2,480 maximum health to match Molt Augmented's Ability Strength bonus at 250 kills.

Patch History

Update 38.6 (2025-05-21)

Arbitration rotation rewards (excluding Omni Forma) have been added to the Arbitration store (Arbiters of Hexis in the Relay) to provide players an easier path to these items via Vitus Essence.
Update 31.6 (2022-06-09)

ARCANE STAT CHANGES PER RANK
We’ve adjusted the values of older Arcanes with the goal of making lower rank Arcanes more viable, and more desirable while collecting as a result. In short, there are three stats that each Arcane tends to have (speaking generally) -- for example, a percentage chance, a stat buff, and the duration of the buff.
Certain Arcanes increase all of these stats per rank (making them scale exponentially), but we are changing it so that only one stat is affected by rank (making it scale linearly). The other stats will now be at the max-rank value regardless of the Arcane Rank, thereby buffing lower-rank Arcanes as a result.

Notes
At all ranks, it will take 50 Health Orbs to reach the maximum Health bonus.
This arcane allows Health Orbs to be picked up without needing to be below full health until the maximum number of stacks is reached.
Unlike most stackable Arcane buffs, Arcane Blessing's bonus persists until death.
Companions with  Link Vitality will have their health increase alongside the Warframe.
Universal Orbs, such as those spawned by  Transmutation Probe, will act as health orbs and provide a stack of bonus health.

History
Arcane Consequence was first introduced in the Trial mission The Jordas Verdict, which was retired in Update 22.14 (2018-03-01).
The Operation: Scarlet Spear event sold Arcane Consequence from Little Duck for 1,000  Scarlet Credits.
The Operation: Orphix Venom event sold Arcane Consequence from Father for 200  Phasic Cells.
The Operation: Gargoyle's Cry event sold Arcane Consequence from the Vigile Jahu Gargoyle for 3  Grotesque Splinter.
The Operation: Belly of the Beast event sold Arcane Consequence from Ordis for 1  Volatile Motes in the first run of the event, and 2 Motes in the second, with a purchase limit of 42.
Notes
The bonus of parkour velocity stacks additively with  Arcane Agility.
Further headshots will refresh the duration of the effect, similarly to  Arcane Rage.
Tips
Can be used with Ivara's  Prowl to significantly increase mobility as the arcane effects roll distance and speed, as well as bullet jumps.

We’ve adjusted the values of older Arcanes with the goal of making lower rank Arcanes more viable, and more desirable while collecting as a result. In short, there are three stats that each Arcane tends to have (speaking generally) -- for example, a percentage chance, a stat buff, and the duration of the buff.
Certain Arcanes increase all of these stats per rank (making them scale exponentially), but we are changing it so that only one stat is affected by rank (making it scale linearly). The other stats will now be at the max-rank value regardless of the Arcane Rank, thereby buffing lower-rank Arcanes as a result.

Arcane Consequence
On Headshot: 100% (was 25%) chance for +10%/60% Parkour Velocity for 18s (was 3s)

Now that the chance is guaranteed, the new description is “On Headshot: +10%/60% Parkour Velocity for 18s”

Notes
Applies to some abilities.
Main article: Universal Weapon Bonuses
Critical damage bonus applies to all equipped weapons, and abilities that can crit, with the exception of  Gyre.
Critical damage multiplier is a flat final and follows the formula 1 + (tier * (crit multi - 1 + final)) where the first tier is yellow (eg. skana multi is 1.5x which is brought to 4.5x (4.5x on yellow, 8x on orange, 11.5x on red and so on) the impact lessens depending on modded critical multiplier)
Will function with  Wisp's passive invisibility while in the air, granting the arcane's effects while she is airborne.  Wil-O-Wisp grants Wisp invisibility while the decoy is active, allowing her to more easily gain the arcane's effects.
Wisp's passive invisibility will work for single shot of non-melee weapons, as Wisp briefly becomes visible after firing any other weapon.
 Nokko can benefit from the Final Critical Multipler part of this arcane if he is in  Reroot at the time critical damage is dealt; however, because Ability Strength is snapshotted at the time an ability is cast, and because abilities cannot be cast during Reroot, the Ability Strength buff cannot be accessed during Reroot.

Notes
To get full stacks, player must perform all 3 actions: Dodge, Double Jump, and Bullet Jump, but they can be in any order.
For example, two Bullet Jumps in a row will only provide one stack of the buff while a Bullet Jump + Dodge roll will provide two stacks.
Each stack is additive with each other (e.g. +75% DR at 3 stacks), but multiplicative with other sources of Damage Reduction.
Each stack has an independent timer.

History
Arcane Energize was first introduced in the Trial mission The Jordas Verdict, which was retired in Update 22.14 (2018-03-01).
The Operation: Scarlet Spear event sold Arcane Energize from Little Duck for 4,000  Scarlet Credits.
The Operation: Orphix Venom event sold Arcane Energize from Father for 800  Phasic Cells.
Nightwave's Season 1 & Intermission 2 rank 25 awarded one unranked Arcane Energize. Nora's Choice rank 26 awarded three unranked Arcane Energize.
The Operation: Gargoyle's Cry event sold Arcane Energize from the Vigile Jahu Gargoyle for 12  Grotesque Splinter.
The Operation: Belly of the Beast event sold Arcane Energize from Ordis for 46  Volatile Motes in the first run of the event, and 30 Motes in the second, with a purchase limit of 42.
The Operation: Eight Claw event sold Arcane Energize from Dominus Thrax in bundles of 3, costing 22  Dominus Aureus, with a purchase limit of 7 (21 Arcanes in total).
Notes
Trading Arcane Energize requires the trader to be Mastery Rank 11 or higher.
Channeled Warframe abilities like  Excalibur's  Exalted Blade and  Banshee's  Sound Quake do not block energy gained from Arcane Energize.
The extra energy from Arcane Energize is not considered as being from an energy orb, and thus will not affect  Equinox's passive,  Equilibrium,  Zenurik's  Energy Pulse, or  Hildryn's shield restore.
Universal Orbs created by  Lavos's  Transmutation Probe can trigger Arcane Energize.
Energy Motes created by  Emergence Dissipate can not trigger Arcane Energize.
Patch History

Update 36.0 (2024-06-18)

Trading Legendary Arcanes now requires you to be Mastery Rank 11.
It’s important to note that only the player trading requires MR 11 and not the receiver.
We needed to address an issue with exploitative Legendary Arcanes farming and ban-evasion account creation used to trade Legendary Arcanes. Other options we experimented with had the risk of innocent players getting caught in the crossfire of the ban-evasion subjects. When we took a look at Trading stats we found that a very small number of players below Mastery Rank 10 trade Legendary Arcanes, and therefore decided to go this route. This is purely a means of deterring the bad actors and ensuring legitimate trading for players.
Update 31.6 (2022-06-09)

ARCANE STAT CHANGES PER RANK
We’ve adjusted the values of older Arcanes with the goal of making lower rank Arcanes more viable, and more desirable while collecting as a result. In short, there are three stats that each Arcane tends to have (speaking generally) -- for example, a percentage chance, a stat buff, and the duration of the buff.
Certain Arcanes increase all of these stats per rank (making them scale exponentially), but we are changing it so that only one stat is affected by rank (making it scale linearly). The other stats will now be at the max-rank value regardless of the Arcane Rank, thereby buffing lower-rank Arcanes as a result.

Notes

Tips
Proc  Impact status to increase health threshold to perform a Mercy Kill.
 Swift Mercy increases speed of Mercy Kill.
Pressing and holding Mercy Kill button allows to perform it at the very moment it becomes available on an enemy, which may be helpful when using high damage weapons to not accidentally kill them before seeing the prompt.
 Coda Bassocyst allows to perform Mercy Kill from distance using alt fire.
There are special interactions with abilities that similarly save from death:
 Dagath's  Grave Spirit,  Nidus' Passive,  Khora's  Venari Bodyguard augment,  Voruna's  Ulfrun's Descent passive,  Valkyr's passive, and Vulpaphyla's  Martyr Symbiosis precept will be triggered first and won't consume Arcane Escapist's charge.
It will still trigger when  Revenant's  Mesmer Skin or  Garuda's  Dread Ward augment are active, though those technically can't receive fatal damage.

History
Arcane Fury was first introduced in the Trial mission The Jordas Verdict, which was retired in Update 22.14 (2018-03-01).
The Operation: Scarlet Spear event sold Arcane Fury from Little Duck for 2,000  Scarlet Credits.
The Operation: Orphix Venom event sold Arcane Fury from Father for 400  Phasic Cells.
The Operation: Gargoyle's Cry event sold Arcane Fury from the Vigile Jahu Gargoyle for 6  Grotesque Splinter.
The Operation: Belly of the Beast event sold Arcane Fury from Ordis for 5  Volatile Motes in the first run of the event, and 6 Motes in the second, with a purchase limit of 42.
The Operation: Eight Claw event sold Arcane Fury from Dominus Thrax in bundles of 7, costing 4  Dominus Aureus, with a purchase limit of 3 (21 Arcanes in total).
Notes
The increase in damage stacks additively with mods that increase base damage e.g.  Pressure Point.
Can be triggered by critical hits from almost any source, including all weapon types, beast and sentinel attacks, and even Warframe abilities (such as  Wisp's  Breach Surge sparks and all of  Gyre's damaging abilities)
Patch History

Update 31.6 (2022-06-09)

ARCANE STAT CHANGES PER RANK
We’ve adjusted the values of older Arcanes with the goal of making lower rank Arcanes more viable, and more desirable while collecting as a result. In short, there are three stats that each Arcane tends to have (speaking generally) -- for example, a percentage chance, a stat buff, and the duration of the buff.
Certain Arcanes increase all of these stats per rank (making them scale exponentially), but we are changing it so that only one stat is affected by rank (making it scale linearly). The other stats will now be at the max-rank value regardless of the Arcane Rank, thereby buffing lower-rank Arcanes as a result.

Arcane Fury
On Critical Hit: 60% (was 10%) chance for +30%/180% Melee Damage to Melee Weapons for 18s (was 3s).
Update 22.12 (2018-02-09)

Now has a base value of 30% with a base chance of 10%.

History
Arcane Grace was first introduced in the Trial mission The Law of Retribution, which was retired in Update 22.14 (2018-03-01).
The Operation: Scarlet Spear event sold Arcane Grace from Little Duck for 4,000  Scarlet Credits.
The Operation: Orphix Venom event sold Arcane Grace from Father for 800  Phasic Cells.
Nightwave's Series 2: The Emissary & Intermission III awarded one Arcane Grace at Rank 27.
The Operation: Gargoyle's Cry event sold Arcane Grace from Jahu Gargoyle for 12  Grotesque Splinter
The Operation: Belly of the Beast event sold Arcane Grace from Ordis for 46  Volatile Motes in the first run of the event, and 30 Motes in the second, with a purchase limit of 42.
The Operation: Eight Claw event sold Arcane Grace from Dominus Thrax in bundles of 3, costing 22  Dominus Aureus, with a purchase limit of 7 (21 Arcanes in total).
Notes
Trading Arcane Grace requires the trader to be Mastery Rank 11 or higher.
Can be triggered with  Combat Discipline.
The amount of health restored refers to health per second, not the total of the duration.
Works best on Warframes with high base Health and Armor, such as  Nidus,  Grendel or  Inaros.
Flat health regeneration like that from  Arcane Reaper may be ideal for Warframes with less maximum Health.
Patch History

Update 36.0 (2024-06-18)

Trading Legendary Arcanes now requires you to be Mastery Rank 11.
It’s important to note that only the player trading requires MR 11 and not the receiver.
We needed to address an issue with exploitative Legendary Arcanes farming and ban-evasion account creation used to trade Legendary Arcanes. Other options we experimented with had the risk of innocent players getting caught in the crossfire of the ban-evasion subjects. When we took a look at Trading stats we found that a very small number of players below Mastery Rank 10 trade Legendary Arcanes, and therefore decided to go this route. This is purely a means of deterring the bad actors and ensuring legitimate trading for players.
Update 35.1 (2024-02-20)

Fixed certain Arcanes (including but not limited to: Arcane Grace) having “0” in their Buff icons instead of the actual stat.
Update 31.6 (2022-06-09)

ARCANE STAT CHANGES PER RANK
We’ve adjusted the values of older Arcanes with the goal of making lower rank Arcanes more viable, and more desirable while collecting as a result. In short, there are three stats that each Arcane tends to have (speaking generally) -- for example, a percentage chance, a stat buff, and the duration of the buff.
Certain Arcanes increase all of these stats per rank (making them scale exponentially), but we are changing it so that only one stat is affected by rank (making it scale linearly). The other stats will now be at the max-rank value regardless of the Arcane Rank, thereby buffing lower-rank Arcanes as a result.

Arcane Grace

Update 30.5 (2021-07-06)

Notes
The additional armor is not affected by mods and is additive to total armor. For example, a  Chroma with a max rank  Steel Fiber, and active Rank 5  Arcane Guardian will have an armor rating of:Base Armor × (1 + Armor Mods) + Arcane Guardian = 370 × (1 + 1.1) + 900 = 1,677 armor.
The Arcane Duration cannot be refreshed, while one instance is still active.
This Arcane cannot be activated while  Revenant's  Mesmer Skin or Righteous Negation (granted by  Oberon's passive) is active.

Patch History

Update 31.6 (2022-06-09)

ARCANE STAT CHANGES PER RANK
We’ve adjusted the values of older Arcanes with the goal of making lower rank Arcanes more viable, and more desirable while collecting as a result. In short, there are three stats that each Arcane tends to have (speaking generally) -- for example, a percentage chance, a stat buff, and the duration of the buff.
Certain Arcanes increase all of these stats per rank (making them scale exponentially), but we are changing it so that only one stat is affected by rank (making it scale linearly). The other stats will now be at the max-rank value regardless of the Arcane Rank, thereby buffing lower-rank Arcanes as a result.

Notes
Critical Chance applies to all weapon types, including primary, secondary, and melee.
Bonus is additive to mods such as  Point Strike,  Pistol Gambit and  True Steel.
Applies as a Universal Weapon Bonus, although most weapon-coded abilities have 0% critical chance, and thus cannot make use of the arcane.
Works with  Minelayer Flechette, but not  Breach Surge.
Exalted Weapons modded with  Heat damage can provide stacks for this arcane (the Atomicycle's  Atomi-Barrage and  Atomi-Bomb can stack too).
All stacks of the buff expire simultaneously at the end of the duration. Applying a new stack refreshes the buff duration.
A  Diriga with  Arc Coil or  Electro Pulse and a Hound with  Synergized Prospectus are able to activate Arcane Hot Shot very reliably with  Manifold Bond and a weapon modded for  Heat.
An important distinction is that stacks are created per infliction of  Heat status, not per proc (i.e., applying two or more procs of Heat at once to one enemy grants only one stack).  Archon Vitality does not provide any benefit for this Arcane.
Trading Arcane Hot Shot requires the trader to be Mastery Rank 11 or higher.

Acquisition
Arcane Ice Storm can be obtained from Ascension's optional Sister of Parvos, summoned by acquiring 3 Sister Beacons during the elevator ride. Alternatively it can be purchased from Ordis in the Drifter's Camp for 10  Vestigial Motes, for a total of 210 for max rank.

Notes
Companion weapons do not trigger this arcane.
This includes companions modded with  Manifold Bond.
All stacks are removed when the duration runs out. All stacks are refreshed upon freezing an enemy.
Doesn't appear to activate on shielded enemies in the Simulacrum.
Tips
Effects that rapidly apply 10  Cold stacks include:
 Frost:
 Freeze's direct hit
 Ice Wave double cast (Helminth subsumable), as each applies 6  Cold stacks
 Snow Globe's activation while within its range
 Avalanche
 Gauss'  Thermal Sunder double cast (Helminth subsumable) or with  Redline active
 Voruna's  Fangs of Raksh (applies 5 random status effects which may include  Cold)
 Lavos's  Vial Rush (Helminth subsumable)
 Follie's  Shadowgraph (Corralizer Armament)
 Itzal's  Cosmic Crush with  Cold Snap augment
 Melee Careen at Rank 5
 Secondary Cryogenic
 Okina's spectral daggers from its incarnon
Enemies are not "frozen" by:
 Atlas's  Petrify
 Harrow's  Condemn, however, the ability can grant Arcane Ice Storm stacks when an affected enemy is damaged by the player. This is most likely an unintended interaction.

Acquisition
May appear as a Steel Path Circuit Tier 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 7, 8, or +11 reward on a weekly rotational basis.
May be sold by Acrithis for 10  Pathos Clamp on a daily rotational basis, requiring Drifter Intrinsics Opportunity Rank 9 for the arcanes to appear in her shop.
One of possible rewards from completing the optional Undercroft Portals in Duviri while on the Steel Path.
Notes
The bonus stacks for every channeled ability simultaneously active, up to a maximum of 4 stacks (+1000 Health at max rank).
Besides increasing maximum health, activating this Arcane also heals the Warframe by the same amount of hitpoints.
After the ability that sustained this Arcane's effect ends, there is a 1 second delay during which said ability cannot be reactivated.

Tips
Many Warframes lack channeled abilities altogether, making it impossible to use this Arcane with them. Certain abilities can be transferred via Helminth to circumvent this:
 Gloom can be infused onto any Warframe in order to give them a channeled ability and enable the use of this Arcane.
 Mind Control and  Parasitic Armor are abilities with high base duration that can maintain this Arcane's effect without constant energy drain.
 Pillage,  Coil Horizon and  Quiver are also Helminth-compatible and can make use of this Arcane's secondary effect of instant healing upon ability activation.

History
Arcane Momentum was first introduced in the Trial mission The Jordas Verdict, which was retired in Update 22.14 (2018-03-01).
The Operation: Scarlet Spear event sold Arcane Momentum from Little Duck for 1,000  Scarlet Credits.
The Operation: Orphix Venom event sold Arcane Momentum from Father for 200  Phasic Cells.
The Operation: Gargoyle's Cry event sold Arcane Momentum from the Vigile Jahu Gargoyle for 3  Grotesque Splinter.
The Operation: Belly of the Beast event sold Arcane Momentum from Ordis for 1  Volatile Motes in the first run of the event, and 2 Motes in the second, with a purchase limit of 42.
Notes
Stacks additively with reload speed from mods such as  Fast Hands.
Can be triggered by critical hits from almost any source, including all weapon types, beast and sentinel attacks, and even Warframe abilities (such as  Wisp's  Breach Surge sparks and all of  Gyre's damaging abilities)
Tips
Using a Sentinel weapon with a high sustained critical hit rate is a good way making use of this Arcane, such as:  Prime Laser Rifle,  Deth Machine Rifle, or  Sweeper Prime.
Sentinels such as Diriga and Djinn can be used for their increased attack range. Alternatively, applying zoom mods like  Eagle Eye to a sentinel's weapon will also increase their attack range.
It's recommended to avoid using mods like  Vicious Spread or  Heavy Caliber as sentinel weapons tend to already have low accuracy.
It should be noted for stealth frames that going invisible will cause sentinels to stop attacking enemies.
Patch History

Hotfix 35.0.3 (2023-12-18)

Fixed the amount of Vosfor received from dissolving Arcane Momentum being lower than intended.
Update 31.6 (2022-06-09)

ARCANE STAT CHANGES PER RANK
We’ve adjusted the values of older Arcanes with the goal of making lower rank Arcanes more viable, and more desirable while collecting as a result. In short, there are three stats that each Arcane tends to have (speaking generally) -- for example, a percentage chance, a stat buff, and the duration of the buff.
Certain Arcanes increase all of these stats per rank (making them scale exponentially), but we are changing it so that only one stat is affected by rank (making it scale linearly). The other stats will now be at the max-rank value regardless of the Arcane Rank, thereby buffing lower-rank Arcanes as a result.

Arcane Momentum
On Critical Hit: 60% (was 10%) chance for +25%/150% Reload Speed to Sniper Rifles for 12s (was 2s).
Hotfix 27.3.12 (2020-04-14)

Fixed Arcane Momentum not activating for Critical shots on the Condrix.
Update 27.2 (2020-03-05)

Added 2 maximum ranks
Maximum chance to activate increased from 40% to 60%
Maximum reload speed increased from +100% to +150%
Update 22.12 (2018-02-09)

Now affects Reload Speed with a base chance of 10%
Hotfix 22.13.1 (2018-02-15)

Fixed Arcane Momentum and Arcane Consequence reflecting incorrect text descriptions since their recent changes in U22.12.0.

Introduced with The Jordas Verdict.

Notes
Directly reduces ammo cost by a percentage, while active. At rank 5 no ammo will be consumed.
After being activated by the secondary, the buff also works on primary weapons.
The buff doesn't affect special weapons, like the Thermian RPG.
Getting another proc does not refresh the timer. The current instance of Arcane Pistoleer must run out before another can be started.
In order for the arcane to activate enemy must be killed with direct damage, Gas or Electric status effects. Other damage over time effects will not trigger the buff.
Alt-fire modes that shoot all remaining bullets in an automatic burst ( Cyanex,  Nagantaka, or  Pandero) shoot the current number of bullets left in the magazine while conserving them, allowing for an ammo-efficient, repeated use of these modes.
 Azima alt-fire still consumes the entire magazine as normal, but the turrets themselves benefit from partial ammo efficiency, expending their ammo more slowly and therefore lasting much longer. However, 100% ammo efficiency does not benefit turrets in any way and they will last their normal duration.
Is also capable of being toggled by pistol Exalted Weapons like the  Dex Pixia.
Tips
Can greatly improve the performance of weapons that otherwise have low magazine size like  Akbronco ( Prime) or  Twin Rogga.
The same applies to primaries like ( Sancti)  Tigris ( Prime), ( Kuva) Tonkor, and  Vectis ( Prime).
Synergizes well with  Pyrana Prime's passive, allowing the wielder to make more use of the fire rate/akimbo buff without sacrificing the buff's duration to reloads.
Patch History

Hotfix 41.0.4 (2025-12-17)

Fixed performance issues caused by the Akarius with Arcane Pistoleer equipped.
Update 38.6 (2025-05-21)

Arbitration rotation rewards (excluding Omni Forma) have been added to the Arbitration store (Arbiters of Hexis in the Relay) to provide players an easier path to these items via Vitus Essence.
Update 31.6 (2022-06-09)

ARCANE STAT CHANGES PER RANK
We’ve adjusted the values of older Arcanes with the goal of making lower rank Arcanes more viable, and more desirable while collecting as a result. In short, there are three stats that each Arcane tends to have (speaking generally) -- for example, a percentage chance, a stat buff, and the duration of the buff.
Certain Arcanes increase all of these stats per rank (making them scale exponentially), but we are changing it so that only one stat is affected by rank (making it scale linearly). The other stats will now be at the max-rank value regardless of the Arcane Rank, thereby buffing lower-rank Arcanes as a result.

Arcane Pistoleer
On Pistol Headshot Kill: 60% (was 10%) chance for +17%/102% Ammo Efficiency for 12s (was 2s)
Update 30.9 (2021-11-11)

Fixed weapons with 1 bullet in their magazine (such as Vectis and Exergis) bypassing the Fire Rate limit if equipped with a max rank Arcane Pistoleer.
Hotfix 25.8.1 (2019-10-09)

Fixed Arcane Pistoleer not giving infinite Ammo at 100% efficiency for weapons that fire more than one bullet at a time.
Hotfix 25.7.7 (2019-09-26)

Fixed Arcane Pistoleer not listing “+1 Arcane Revive” at max Rank.
Hotfix 25.7.6 (2019-09-18)

Introduced.
